text: TIL Tighnari Is The Only Genshin Character So Far Designed by an Outsourced Artist. And that artist was the Japanese illustrator pako, who also designed a bunch of characters across the Fate franchise. Honestly, back then Hoyo's 3D model didn't fully do justice to the vibrancy and uniqueness of pako's original 2D design. I think that's partly why a lot of people felt Tighnari looked kinda messy or out of place when he first came out. If Tighnari had released during the Natlan era, when character models started becoming much more detailed and visually complex, things honestly might have turned out differently. But with how Genshin character designs have been getting more criticism lately, especially people saying designs are becoming too same-y, I wonder if Hoyo would ever give outside artists another shot. Personally, I really liked Tighnari's design back then because it felt genuinely unique compared to the rest of the cast. I'd love to see the game keep experimenting more and give us more characters with bold, unconventional designs that stand out from the crowd.
label: lore_analysis
notes: Shares a factual piece of game development info and reasons about its implications - qualifies as lore/design analysis with supporting reasoning

text: Can we talk about how fontaine is literally a dictatorship now. I've been rewatching the end of the Fontaine Archon quest, and the more you look at the actual lore, the worse it gets. Focalors didn't save Fontaine's political system. She accidentally set up an absolute autocracy... Think about how the government actually worked before: You had the Hydro Archon as the executive face, Neuvillette running the judicial branch, and the Oratrice acting as a literal machine check-and-balance. The Oratrice could, and did completely overrule Neuvillette's personal verdicts, like it did with Tartaglia. There was a separation of powers. But look at what the ending actually did: The Oratrice is completely destroyed. Neuvillette is now the final judge, jury, and executioner... Focalors didn't democratize Fontaine. She just replaced a fake god with an all-powerful monarch and we all ignored the political horror of it because the cutscene was sad.
label: lore_analysis
notes: Textbook lore_analysis: makes a structured argument about Fontaine's political system with specific textual evidence from the game
